title: Kinematics Quiz
description: Test your understanding of motion, velocity, and acceleration

questions.text: A car travels 100 km in 2 hours. What is its average velocity?
An object starts from rest and accelerates at 5 m/s². How far does it travel in 4 seconds?
What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent?
A ball is thrown vertically upward with initial velocity 20 m/s. How long until it returns to the starting height? (g=10 m/s²)
Which of these is NOT a vector quantity?
A cyclist travels 15 m in 3 seconds. What is the velocity in m/s?
In uniform circular motion, the velocity is tangent to the circle and the acceleration points toward the center. This acceleration is called:
An object with constant velocity has acceleration equal to:
A projectile is launched horizontally at 20 m/s from a 45 m tall cliff. How long does it take to hit the ground? (g=10 m/s²)
Which graph best represents motion with constant acceleration?
